added_content.ex_information:
Choose
TWO
letters,
A–E
.

obj.content: Which [b] TWO [/b] things does the speaker say about visiting the football stadium with children?
obj.html_content: Which TWO things does the speaker say about visiting the football stadium with children?

obj.content: Children can get their photo taken with a football player.
obj.html_content: Children can get their photo taken with a football player.

obj.content: There is a competition for children today.
obj.html_content: There is a competition for children today.

obj.content: Parents must stay with their children at all times.
obj.html_content: Parents must stay with their children at all times.

obj.content: Children will need sunhats and drinks.
obj.html_content: Children will need sunhats and drinks.

obj.content: The café has a special offer on meals for children.
obj.html_content: The café has a special offer on meals for children.

sentence.content: Good morning, and welcome to City Football Club.
sentence.content_zh: 大家早上好，欢迎来到城市足球俱乐部。

sentence.content: I’d like to give you some useful information about your visit to the stadium today, and then we’ll start the tour of the areas of the stadium that are open to visitors.
sentence.content_zh: 在开始参观今天对外开放的场馆之前，请允许我向大家介绍一些实用信息。

sentence.content: I can see lots of children here today, so just to let mums and dads know a few things before we start.
sentence.content_zh: 我看到今天有很多小朋友在场，因此先请各位家长注意几个事项。

sentence.content: The stadium has lots of stairs, and the players’ tunnel is very dark.
sentence.content_zh: 体育场内楼梯很多，球员通道也非常昏暗。

sentence.content: Please don’t let your children wander off on their own, even for a minute. We don’t want any accidents or anyone getting frightened.
sentence.content_zh: 请不要让孩子单独离开身边，哪怕只是一分钟也不行。我们不希望发生任何意外，也不想让孩子受到惊吓。

sentence.content: Cameras are permitted everywhere, and you can take pictures of your child shooting a penalty.
sentence.content_zh: 整个场馆都可以拍照，你们可以拍下孩子罚点球的照片留作纪念。

sentence.content: Assistants are helping to organise this, and hopefully the queue won’t be too long – it’s very hot and sunny out on the pitch today.
sentence.content_zh: 有工作人员在现场协助组织拍照活动，希望排队的时间不会太长——毕竟今天场地外面阳光猛烈，天气非常热。

sentence.content: You can get food and drink at the café, and I really recommend the healthy lunch boxes for children.
sentence.content_zh: 你们可以在咖啡馆用餐和购买饮料，我特别推荐为孩子们准备的健康午餐盒。

sentence.content: Also in the café, children are invited to do a football-themed drawing.
sentence.content_zh: 此外，在咖啡馆里还邀请小朋友们参加以足球为主题的绘画活动。

sentence.content: We’ll pick the best one at the end of the afternoon, so don’t forget to put your name and contact details on the back.
sentence.content_zh: 我们会在下午结束前评选出最棒的作品，所以记得在画纸背面写上你的名字和联系方式。

sentence.content: That way, if you’ve left the stadium before then, we’ll send your prize, but sadly we can’t return drawings.
sentence.content_zh: 这样即使你们在公布结果前已经离开场馆，我们也可以把奖品寄给你，不过很抱歉，画作我们无法归还。

sentence.content: I’d like to mention some features of the tour.
sentence.content_zh: 接下来我想介绍一下本次参观的一些安排。

sentence.content: We’ll start with the three-sixty cinema experience, which has been very popular over the years, and then I’ll take you to the players’ dressing rooms before going outside to the seating area and the pitch.
sentence.content_zh: 我们将首先体验“360度电影院”，这个项目多年来一直广受好评。接着我们会参观球员更衣室，然后到室外的观众席和球场区域。

sentence.content: I should say, if you’d prefer your visit to be self-guided, please collect headphones from the reception and then you can listen to the pre-recorded information at your own speed.
sentence.content_zh: 另外需要说明的是，如果你希望自由安排参观节奏，可以前往前台领取耳机，收听预先录制好的讲解内容。

sentence.content: We’ve only just introduced this feature, and would appreciate your feedback.
sentence.content_zh: 这项服务是最近才推出的，我们非常欢迎你们提出反馈意见。

sentence.content: We’re thinking of offering tours in other languages in future so if you have any thoughts on that, we’d welcome those too.
sentence.content_zh: 如果未来我们考虑提供其他语言的导览服务，也欢迎大家对此提出建议。

sentence.content: If you plan to return another time, you might like to book one of our VIP tours – we’ve only just started offering these, and they can be booked online.
sentence.content_zh: 如果你打算以后再来参观，也可以提前预订我们的贵宾导览项目——这种服务也是最近刚推出的，可以通过网络进行预订。

sentence.content: Now, the stadium you see today was built in 1989, as part of a three-year redevelopment project
sentence.content_zh: 现在大家看到的这座体育场建于1989年，是为期三年的重建项目的一部分。

sentence.content: while that project was going on, the team had to play its matches at the ground of another club – apart from that, the club has been here on this site since 1870.
sentence.content_zh: 在那期间，球队不得不借用另一家俱乐部的场地进行比赛。除此之外，自1870年以来，俱乐部就一直位于这片场地上。

sentence.content: As some of you may know, that was the start of a really important decade in the history of football in this country.
sentence.content_zh: 可能有些朋友知道，1870年正是我国足球历史上一个非常重要的十年的开端。

sentence.content: For example, 1870 was also the year that football teams started to include a player whose role it was to guard the goal.
sentence.content_zh: 例如，就在这一年，足球队中首次出现了专门负责守门的球员角色。

sentence.content: It’s hard to imagine what the game must have been like without someone in that position, isn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: 很难想象当时没有守门员的情况下比赛是什么样子，对吧？

sentence.content: In 1872 and 73, many other clubs were established, both here and abroad, and the following year, in 1874, referees were allowed to send players off if they committed certain offences.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1872年和1873年，许多其他的俱乐部陆续成立，不仅是在国内，国外也开始出现足球俱乐部。紧接着在1874年，裁判被授权可以在某些违规情况下将球员罚出场外。

sentence.content: And also in that year, teams started having to swap ends at half-time.
sentence.content_zh: 同样是在那一年，比赛规定上下半场之间必须交换场地。

sentence.content: One fact I was interested to discover was that in early football games, the aim was for the scorer to get the ball between two flag posts, and later between sticks joined at the top with a piece of tape.
sentence.content_zh: 我还发现了一个有意思的历史事实：早期的足球比赛中，目标是让进球者把球射入两个旗杆之间的区域，后来则是用一根连接两根立柱顶部的带子作为球门。

sentence.content: In 1875, that tape was replaced with the solid crossbar that we’re familiar with today.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1875年，这根带子就被我们现在熟悉的坚固横梁所取代了。

sentence.content: 1877 saw the founding of further new clubs, and the history books tell us that in the same year, all the clubs decided to set a limit of ninety minutes for each match.
sentence.content_zh: 1877年又建立了更多的新俱乐部，历史资料记载，同年各俱乐部达成一致，正式把每场比赛的时间定为90分钟。

sentence.content: Before that, it was a more casual arrangement, and this sometimes caused huge arguments – and sometimes fights – during matches, when one team called the end of the game and the other team wanted to play on to try and score a winning goal.
sentence.content_zh: 在此之前，比赛时间比较随意，有时还会引发巨大的争执，甚至打架——因为一方球队认为比赛已经结束了，另一方却还想继续踢下去，试图进制胜一球。

sentence.content: By 1878, the number of teams in the football league increased again.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1878年，足球联赛中的球队数量再次增加。

sentence.content: In addition, referees started using whistles, and electric lamps were installed on certain pitches.
sentence.content_zh: 此外，裁判开始使用哨子，部分球场也安装了电灯。

sentence.content: This was a significant change, as games could then be played in the evenings all year round.
sentence.content_zh: 这一变化非常重大，因为从此之后，全年都可以在晚上进行比赛了。

sentence.content: In 1880, clubs began to charge fans for admission to games, even though players were still amateurs and had other ‘proper’ jobs.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1880年，俱乐部开始对球迷收取入场费用，尽管那时的球员仍然是业余选手，还有其他的“正式”工作。

sentence.content: That’s hard to imagine in the modern professional game where top players earn significant sums of money from both playing and commercial activities.
sentence.content_zh: 这在如今的职业足球世界里简直难以想象——现在的顶级球员不仅靠踢球获得可观收入，还能通过商业活动赚取大量报酬。

.

obj.content: Which [b] TWO[/b] features of the stadium tour are new this year?
obj.html_content: Which TWO features of the stadium tour are new this year?

obj.content: VIP tour
obj.html_content: VIP tour

obj.content: 360 cinema experience
obj.html_content: 360 cinema experience

obj.content: audio guide
obj.html_content: audio guide

obj.content: dressing room tour
obj.html_content: dressing room tour

obj.content: tours in other languages
obj.html_content: tours in other languages

sentence.content_zh: 接下来我想介绍一下本次参观的一些安排。

sentence.content: We’ll start with the three-sixty cinema experience, which has been very popular over the years, and then I’ll take you to the players’ dressing rooms before going outside to the seating area and the pitch.
sentence.content_zh: 我们将首先体验“360度电影院”，这个项目多年来一直广受好评。接着我们会参观球员更衣室，然后到室外的观众席和球场区域。

sentence.content: I should say, if you’d prefer your visit to be self-guided, please collect headphones from the reception and then you can listen to the pre-recorded information at your own speed.
sentence.content_zh: 另外需要说明的是，如果你希望自由安排参观节奏，可以前往前台领取耳机，收听预先录制好的讲解内容。

sentence.content: We’ve only just introduced this feature, and would appreciate your feedback.
sentence.content_zh: 这项服务是最近才推出的，我们非常欢迎你们提出反馈意见。

sentence.content: We’re thinking of offering tours in other languages in future so if you have any thoughts on that, we’d welcome those too.
sentence.content_zh: 如果未来我们考虑提供其他语言的导览服务，也欢迎大家对此提出建议。

sentence.content: If you plan to return another time, you might like to book one of our VIP tours – we’ve only just started offering these, and they can be booked online.
sentence.content_zh: 如果你打算以后再来参观，也可以提前预订我们的贵宾导览项目——这种服务也是最近刚推出的，可以通过网络进行预订。

sentence.content: Now, the stadium you see today was built in 1989, as part of a three-year redevelopment project
sentence.content_zh: 现在大家看到的这座体育场建于1989年，是为期三年的重建项目的一部分。

sentence.content: while that project was going on, the team had to play its matches at the ground of another club – apart from that, the club has been here on this site since 1870.
sentence.content_zh: 在那期间，球队不得不借用另一家俱乐部的场地进行比赛。除此之外，自1870年以来，俱乐部就一直位于这片场地上。

sentence.content: As some of you may know, that was the start of a really important decade in the history of football in this country.
sentence.content_zh: 可能有些朋友知道，1870年正是我国足球历史上一个非常重要的十年的开端。

sentence.content: For example, 1870 was also the year that football teams started to include a player whose role it was to guard the goal.
sentence.content_zh: 例如，就在这一年，足球队中首次出现了专门负责守门的球员角色。

sentence.content: It’s hard to imagine what the game must have been like without someone in that position, isn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: 很难想象当时没有守门员的情况下比赛是什么样子，对吧？

sentence.content: In 1872 and 73, many other clubs were established, both here and abroad, and the following year, in 1874, referees were allowed to send players off if they committed certain offences.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1872年和1873年，许多其他的俱乐部陆续成立，不仅是在国内，国外也开始出现足球俱乐部。紧接着在1874年，裁判被授权可以在某些违规情况下将球员罚出场外。

sentence.content: And also in that year, teams started having to swap ends at half-time.
sentence.content_zh: 同样是在那一年，比赛规定上下半场之间必须交换场地。

sentence.content: One fact I was interested to discover was that in early football games, the aim was for the scorer to get the ball between two flag posts, and later between sticks joined at the top with a piece of tape.
sentence.content_zh: 我还发现了一个有意思的历史事实：早期的足球比赛中，目标是让进球者把球射入两个旗杆之间的区域，后来则是用一根连接两根立柱顶部的带子作为球门。

sentence.content: In 1875, that tape was replaced with the solid crossbar that we’re familiar with today.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1875年，这根带子就被我们现在熟悉的坚固横梁所取代了。

sentence.content: 1877 saw the founding of further new clubs, and the history books tell us that in the same year, all the clubs decided to set a limit of ninety minutes for each match.
sentence.content_zh: 1877年又建立了更多的新俱乐部，历史资料记载，同年各俱乐部达成一致，正式把每场比赛的时间定为90分钟。

sentence.content: Before that, it was a more casual arrangement, and this sometimes caused huge arguments – and sometimes fights – during matches, when one team called the end of the game and the other team wanted to play on to try and score a winning goal.
sentence.content_zh: 在此之前，比赛时间比较随意，有时还会引发巨大的争执，甚至打架——因为一方球队认为比赛已经结束了，另一方却还想继续踢下去，试图进制胜一球。

sentence.content: By 1878, the number of teams in the football league increased again.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1878年，足球联赛中的球队数量再次增加。

sentence.content: In addition, referees started using whistles, and electric lamps were installed on certain pitches.
sentence.content_zh: 此外，裁判开始使用哨子，部分球场也安装了电灯。

sentence.content: This was a significant change, as games could then be played in the evenings all year round.
sentence.content_zh: 这一变化非常重大，因为从此之后，全年都可以在晚上进行比赛了。

sentence.content: In 1880, clubs began to charge fans for admission to games, even though players were still amateurs and had other ‘proper’ jobs.
sentence.content_zh: 到了1880年，俱乐部开始对球迷收取入场费用，尽管那时的球员仍然是业余选手，还有其他的“正式”工作。

sentence.content: That’s hard to imagine in the modern professional game where top players earn significant sums of money from both playing and commercial activities.
sentence.content_zh: 这在如今的职业足球世界里简直难以想象——现在的顶级球员不仅靠踢球获得可观收入，还能通过商业活动赚取大量报酬。

officialExplain.explain:
本节原文的开场介绍中，先提及城市足球俱乐部及足球场，后续内容中children原词重现，并讲到a few things，对应题干问题中的信息。在这部分内容中，从let mums and dads know可以看出是面向家长们，同时对应选项中的Parents。

C：原文中说don’t let your children wander off on their own，your children表现出C选项中的Parents，wander意为“走失，闲逛”，嘱咐家长不要让孩子们自己到处闲逛走散了，也就是C中表达的，家长们应当和孩子们待在一起。原文中don’t…以及后续内容even for a minute，对应C中的all times。因此C为正确选项之一。

A：原文中的Cameras和take pictures对应A选项中的get their photo taken…，但相关表述是you can take pictures of your child shooting a penalty，you指parents，家长们可以拍摄孩子射点球的照片，后续内容还提及Assistants，都未涉及到A中的with a football player，排除A。

D：原文提及hot and sunny，以及food and drink，看似对应D选项中的drinks，但未提及需要遮阳帽sunhats，排除D。

E：原文中café原词重现，相关内容讲到食物和饮料，以及推荐健康餐，但未涉及E选项中的特价销售special offer，排除E。

B：B选项中的children和today在原文中都原词重现。讲到café后，原文提及a football-themed drawing，可以理解为“以足球为主题的绘画”。后续内容中的pick the best和prize对应B选项中的competition。因此B为正确选项之一。

因此11~12题答案为BC。

本节内容中需要特别注意题干中new这一限定信息。原文中features原词重现，对应题干问题中的信息。

B：B选项内容在原文中原词重现，但相关信息为over the years，不符合题干中的new，排除B。

D：原文中的dressing rooms对应D选项，但未出现对应题干new的表述，排除D。

C：原文中讲到self-guided，以及后续讲到的headphones和listen to the pre-recorded information，对应C选项中的audio guide。headphones意为“耳机”，pre-record表示“预先录制”，audio guide可以理解为“语音导览”。后续内容中only just introduced this feature对应题干中的new，only just表示“刚刚”。因此C为正确选项之一。

E：E选项内容在原文中原词重现，但相关信息为in future，不符合题干中的new，排除E。

A：原文中的VIP tours对应A选项，之后讲到only just started，对应题干中的new。因此A为正确选项之一。

因此13~14答案为AC。
原文中1870原词重现，对应本题题干。原文的started to include对应D选项的introduction，player和guard the goal对应选项的goalkeepers，goal在这里意为“球门”，goalkeeper表示“守门员”。因此答案为D。

原文中1874原词重现，对应本题题干。这部分原文中以And also连接了两方面规则变化：一是1874和And also出现前讲到的裁判职权相关内容，referees were allowed to send players off if they committed certain offences；二是And also出现后讲到的中场交换场地相关内容，teams started having to swap ends at half time。这些对应了比赛规则相关的两方面变化，因此答案为F。可以结合文本解析3进一步理解这部分原文。

原文中1875原词重现，对应本题题干。这段原文描述的是球赛中的球门演变：先提及early football games，也就是早期的球赛中，将球踢入两根旗杆之间即得分，可以理解为两根旗杆two flag posts代表的就是球门；结合and later，讲到后来两根杆演变为顶端由带子tape连接的球门形式；在1875年替换为球门横梁crossbar。因此答案为B，选项中的goal意为“球门”。可以结合文本解析4进一步理解这部分原文。
原文中1877原词重现，结合the same year对应本题题干。原文中all the clubs decided对应H选项中的agreement，比赛时长ninety minutes对应length，match对应game。因此答案为H。
原文中1878原词重现，对应本题题干。与1878相关的原文中，先讲到球队数量增加，in addition之后的内容中，讲到裁判开始使用哨子，以及一些球场安装了电灯。原文中的lamps对应C选项的lights。后续内容中还提及是意义重大的变化，因为之后也可以在晚间进行比赛了，这也反映出灯在比赛中的首次使用。因此答案为C。
原文中1880原词重现，对应本题题干。原文中began to可以对应G选项中的introduction，charge…for admission对应fee，fans对应spectators。其中，admission表示“入场费，进入权”，fan可以理解为“球迷”，spectator意为“观众”。因此答案为G。
